name: trend_join_long_scanner
description: Spec for the Trend Join Long day-trading entry scanner. Driven inside a Claude Code session that has the TradingView MCP; not runnable by a bare shell or the standalone CLI (no MCP there).

prereq.on_fail: Push a one-line alert telling the user to launch TradingView with: open -a TradingView --args --remote-debugging-port=9222, then stop.

time_gate.note: The loop further restricts firing to 10:00-14:00 weekdays (see loop.window).

steps: chart_set_timeframe('D'); data_get_ohlcv(count=210) -> prev_daily_high=last bar high; prev_daily_close=last bar close; sma200=mean(close of last 200 bars)
chart_set_timeframe('1'); data_get_ohlcv(count=400) -> pmh=max high where today 04:00<=t<09:30 ET; today_hod=max high where today 09:30<=t<now, excluding current bar

evaluate.result: PASS if both true; else fail_daily if daily_breakout false; else fail_intraday

output.print: one line per ticker: 'TICKER: result — reason'

loop.mechanism: session-bound durable cron (.claude/scheduled_tasks.json); fires only while a Claude Code session is open and idle

ping_when: a NEW PASS symbol not already in today's hits_seen
notification.otherwise: stay silent (still save the watchlist file and update state)
